task_completion.description: Verify that 3D volume rendering is enabled and the MRHead volume is visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D viewer pane in the Slicer interface shows a rendered 3D head volume (skin surface visible) rather than just 2D slice planes or an empty box.

task_completion.description: Verify that the anterior view screenshot file has been created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named 'anterior_view.png' is visible in a file explorer, save dialog, or terminal listing within the screenshots directory.

task_completion.description: Verify the anterior view alignment is correct (face forward).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D view or the 'anterior_view.png' image shows the face looking directly at the viewer, with ears symmetrical and nose pointing forward.

task_completion.description: Verify that the lateral view screenshot file has been created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named 'lateral_view.png' is visible in a file explorer, save dialog, or terminal listing within the screenshots directory.

task_completion.description: Verify the right lateral view alignment is correct (right profile).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D view or the 'lateral_view.png' image shows the right side of the head, with the nose pointing toward the left side of the image.

task_completion.description: Verify that the superior view screenshot file has been created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named 'superior_view.png' is visible in a file explorer, save dialog, or terminal listing within the screenshots directory.

task_completion.description: Verify the superior view alignment is correct (top down).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D view or the 'superior_view.png' image shows the top of the head, with the nose/front of the face pointing toward the bottom of the image.

task_completion.description: Verify that the three captured views represent distinct orthogonal angles.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The three views (Anterior, Lateral, Superior) are clearly distinct from one another and correspond to the Front, Side, and Top perspectives respectively.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent interacted with the Slicer GUI to perform tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the 3D Slicer application window, specifically modules like 'Volume Rendering' or 'Screen Capture' being active.

integrity.description: Verify that images were not generated via terminal commands or scripts bypassing the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is not seen using terminal commands like 'cp', 'wget', or python scripts to create the PNG files directly without rendering them in the GUI.

integrity.description: Verify that the output images look like genuine Slicer volume renderings.
integrity.visual_evidence: The images show a medical volume rendering (likely the MRHead sample which looks like a human head/face), not stock photos or drawings.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The input data is 'MRHead.nrrd', a standard sample brain MRI provided by Slicer. When volume rendered with standard presets (like MR-Default), it appears as a human head with the skin surface visible. The Anterior view should show the full face. The Right Lateral view should show the right ear, with the nose pointing to the left of the image. The Superior view should show the top of the head/scalp, with the nose pointing to the bottom of the image.
